newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. The headline $2.4B Base Electron / Applied Digital full-NTP power project was announced March 4, 2026, and a securities-fraud class action alleging concealed contract risks was filed in May 2026 - both predate the window. The +6.3% move since June 9 is price/sentiment, not fresh fundamentals.

sources.label: B&W full NTP on $2.4B Base Electron project (Mar 4, 2026)
B&W securities fraud lawsuit over $2.4B AI contract

verdicts.rankRationale: Power systems / thermal infrastructure fit intact. The $2.4B Base Electron data-center power deal (Mar 4) and the related securities suit both predate the window; nothing new June 9-23. Hold rank on the electricity scaling basket; +6.3% move is price-only.
verdicts.bull: 1.2GW Base Electron NTP plus prior 1GW Applied Digital give multi-year AI-power backlog visibility into 2028.
verdicts.neutral: Order book is known; execution and a pending fraud suit cap incremental upside this window.
verdicts.bear: Securities class action alleging concealed contract risks; design-build execution and gas-turbine supply chain risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI data-center power backlog conversion to revenue
